pi_items.source: Verified against setup_task.sh line 12 and export_result.sh line 12; task.json description specifies saving submarine at ocean floor to this path
Verified against setup_task.sh line 13 and export_result.sh line 13; task.json description specifies saving submarine at surface to this path
Verified against setup_task.sh line 14 and export_result.sh line 14; task.json description requires captain's log file at this path

Verified through verifier.py line 34 and cross-referenced with GCompris submarine physics activity documentation. All keywords are semantically appropriate: ballast/tank/valve relate to submarine mechanics, density/sink/rise/float relate to buoyancy principles, water is the medium.
verifier.py lines 58 and 67 enforce minimum size: `depth_info.get('size', 0) > 5000` and `surf_info.get('size', 0) > 5000`

GCompris documentation and GitHub repository confirm submarine activity exists in Science category. Task description mentions 'Science/Discovery category' but GCompris documentation and source code indicate 'Science' is the standard category name.
task_utils.sh line 33 states 'GCompris 2.3 does NOT support --launch flag' indicating knowledge of version 2.3. Web search confirms GCompris 2.3 includes submarine activity.

privileged_info_summary: The submarine_buoyancy_pilot task in GCompris is a verified educational activity that teaches submarine buoyancy physics using ballast tank mechanics. The task requires agents to navigate GCompris's Submarine activity (in the Science category) to reach the ocean floor, capture a screenshot at depth, return to the surface, capture another screenshot, and write a captain's log explaining the physics using keywords: ballast, density, sink, rise, float, tank, water, and valve. All file paths (/home/ga/Documents/submarine_depth.png, /home/ga/Documents/submarine_surface.png, /home/ga/Documents/captains_log.txt) are verified and consistent across setup, export, and verifier scripts. GCompris 2.3 includes the Submarine activity, confirmed through web search and KDE source repository documentation. The verifier enforces: (1) both screenshots must exist and be created during task window with size > 5000 bytes, (2) log file must contain at least 2 of the 8 required physics keywords in at least 20 characters of content, and (3) VLM verification of trajectory and screenshots. All data is synthetic, generated during task execution within the simulated Ubuntu environment with user 'ga'.
